events.title: US and Iran Announce Deal to End Conflict, Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran have announced a deal to end their recent conflict, with a commitment to the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts. A key component of the agreement is the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for global oil transport. This development has led to a drop in oil prices and a rise in stock markets.

events.title: Trump States Iran Will Not Possess Nuclear Weapon Under New Deal
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump asserted that a memorandum of understanding with Iran clearly states Tehran will not develop nuclear weapons. He indicated that the text of this agreement would be formally released and suggested the possibility of submitting the deal to Congress for review, a move sought by some Republican lawmakers.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes Target Hezbollah in Beirut Amidst Deal Uncertainty
events.desc: The Israeli military reported conducting air strikes on targets associated with Hezbollah in Beirut. Iran issued a warning that such actions could jeopardize the recently announced US-Iran deal to end the fighting. This exchange highlights the volatile security situation and the potential for de-escalation to falter.

events.title: Families Return to Damaged Homes in Southern Lebanon Following Ceasefire
events.desc: Thousands of Lebanese are returning to their homes in southern Lebanon, which sustained significant damage during recent hostilities. The return occurs despite Israel indicating it would not end its occupation of Lebanon. The fragile ceasefire appears to be holding, but the long-term impact of the destruction is severe.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Confirms Widespread Demolition in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Recent satellite imagery reveals extensive destruction in southern Lebanon, with at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israel Defense Forces' 'Yellow Line' being heavily damaged or completely flattened. While a ceasefire is reportedly holding, the scale of demolition indicates significant recent conflict.

events.title: Iran Executes Two Individuals Over January 2026 Unrest
events.desc: Iran's judiciary news outlet Mizan reported the execution of two individuals, identified as Javad Zamani and Abolfazl Saedi, in the county of Shahrud. They were convicted of charges including 'waging war against God' and 'corruption on earth' stemming from their alleged roles in protests in early 2026.

events.title: Israeli Court Upholds Detention of Palestinian Doctor Without Charge
events.desc: Israel's Supreme Court rejected an appeal for the release of a prominent Palestinian doctor, Hussam Abu Safiya, who has been held without charge since his capture in Gaza in late 2024. He is among at least 14 Gaza doctors detained in Israel under similar circumstances.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel Trade Strikes Amidst Ceasefire Efforts
events.desc: Following intensified fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, both sides have engaged in direct strikes. While a US-Iran deal has been announced to end the broader conflict, recent escalations, including Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon that reportedly killed 17 people, underscore the precariousness of the situation.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: The US military struck a Palau-flagged oil tanker, resulting in the deaths of three Indian sailors. The US stated the action was taken after the tanker failed to comply with directions. This incident adds to maritime security concerns in the region.

events.title: Turkey Urges Russia to Avoid Actions Threatening Black Sea Security
events.desc: Turkey's foreign minister communicated to his Russian counterpart the necessity of avoiding actions in the Black Sea that could jeopardize regional security and Turkish interests. Turkey reiterated its offer to mediate between Ukraine and Russia, amidst ongoing tensions and accusations of drone attacks.

events.title: US-Iran Deal's Impact on Lebanon Remains Unclear
events.desc: While Pakistan announced that the US-Iran deal includes Lebanon, the effectiveness of recent ceasefires in the region has been limited. The inclusion of Lebanon in the broader agreement suggests an attempt to de-escalate regional tensions, but recent events indicate challenges in achieving lasting peace.

events.title: Israel Seizes Control of Hebron Shrine Powers from Palestinian Authority
events.desc: Israel has revoked the Palestinian Authority's planning and construction powers at a shrine in Hebron, a site sacred to both Jews and Muslims. This move nullifies an agreement from the 1990s, altering the management of the Tomb of the Patriarchs and the Ibrahimi Mosque.

events.title: Banned Russian Submunitions Discovered in Mali After Airstrikes
events.desc: Unexploded cluster munition bomblets, identified as Russian-made and banned under international convention, have been found in northern Mali. Their discovery follows an announcement of airstrikes by Mali's military, raising concerns about the use of prohibited weapons.

events.title: Iran-US Deal Triggers Debate on Regional Factional Politics
events.desc: Factional divisions within Iran are surfacing as leaders discuss the implementation of a deal with the US. Hardliners and moderates are reportedly debating the agreement, with concerns raised about potential capitulation. This internal debate highlights the complex political landscape in Iran.

events.title: US-Iran Deal Announced, Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
events.desc: A comprehensive deal has been reached between the US and Iran to end the recent conflict, with both sides declaring an 'immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts.' President Donald Trump confirmed that the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened as part of the agreement.
